title: Tom Brady busy at Fanatics Fest, from a face slap to showing off his arm
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tom Brady slapped Logan Paul during a Fanatics Fest panel in NYC, sparking viral reactions.
The slap followed a running feud after a flag football game where Paul's team beat Brady's.
Brady also engaged in activities like pitching a baseball and golfing at the event.

title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men charged with kidnapping and assault near Winter Park on Thursday afternoon.
Victim found tied with an extension cord, suffering from facial injuries, after reported playground attack.
Suspects identified as Calray Brown, Dijon Hazel, Newton Lawrence, and Alvin Barnett, released on bail pending arraignment.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Maria Rosales and David Ardila, both with pending immigration cases, were arrested by ICE at Logan Airport.
Rosales was protected under DACA, while Ardila was awaiting asylum; both face legal challenges over their detention.
ICE increased targeted operations at Logan, with approximately 10 immigration-related arrests reported over three months.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Kevin Slane suggests five films to watch after Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey', highlighting thematic similarities.
'Interstellar' and 'Lawrence of Arabia' are among the recommended films sharing parallels with 'The Odyssey.'
The list includes films like 'The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie' and 'The Searchers', drawing from Homeric themes.

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox extend their win streak to 11 games after sweeping the Tampa Bay Rays in a doubleheader.
Game 1 featured a 10-0 victory, with Jake Bennett pitching six scoreless innings and Boston's lineup hitting double-digit runs.
After the sweep, the Red Sox improved to a .500 season and hold the third wild-card spot in the American League.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ranger Suarez and Connelly Early, both recent IL additions, show significant recovery progress and could return soon.
Kutter Crawford, Tanner Houck, and Johan Oviedo are making strides in their rehabilitation with potential season returns.
Players like Roman Anthony and Garrett Crochet remain shut down, with no updates on their recovery timetables.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit three times, including a home run, helping the Red Sox win against the Rays in Game 1.
Caleb Durbin went 5 for 7, applying pressure on the Rays' pitching and boosting his batting effectiveness.
Jake Bennett pitched six scoreless innings, helping preserve the Red Sox bullpen for Game 2 against the Rays.

title: Robert Kraft backs Mike Vrabel after controversial offseason: ‘No one is infallible’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Robert Kraft supports Patriots coach Mike Vrabel after offseason controversy involving alleged affair with reporter Dianna Russini.
Vrabel missed Day 3 of the NFL draft amid personal issues but remains Patriots head coach as training camp approaches.
New England Patriots made the Super Bowl last season, Vrabel's first as head coach, after a previous 4-13 season.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "The Hunt for the Wilderpeople" featuring Sam Neill is streaming on Prime Video and Plex.
"Obsession," a 2026 horror hit directed by Curry Barker, available on Peacock.
Apple TV features "Lucky," starring Anya Taylor-Joy, about a crime family's adventures.

title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Travis Lufkin, 25, violated NH Civil Rights Act after assaulting a transgender woman in Concord in May 2024.
Lufkin struck the victim at a Speedway convenience store, motivated by bias against her gender identity.
Court imposed a $5,000 fine on Lufkin and ordered him to avoid contact with the victim for three years.
